164	export class Pipeline extends Callable {
165	    /**
166	     * Create a new Pipeline.
167	     * @param {Object} options An object containing the following properties:
168	     * @param {string} [options.task] The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
169	     * @param {PreTrainedModel} [options.model] The model used by the pipeline.
170	     * @param {PreTrainedTokenizer} [options.tokenizer=null] The tokenizer used by the pipeline (if any).
171	     * @param {Processor} [options.processor=null] The processor used by the pipeline (if any).
172	     */
173	    constructor({ task, model, tokenizer = null, processor = null }) {
174	        super();
175	        this.task = task;
176	        this.model = model;
177	        this.tokenizer = tokenizer;
178	        this.processor = processor;
179	    }
180	
181	    /** @type {DisposeType} */
182	    async dispose() {
183	        await this.model.dispose();
184	    }
185	}

2733	
2734	    /**
2735	     * Create a new DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline.
2736	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
2737	     */
2738	    constructor(options) {
2739	        super(options);
2740	    }
2741	
2742	    /** @type {DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback} */
2743	    async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {}) {
2744	
2745	        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1
2746	
2747	        // Preprocess image
2748	        const preparedImage = (await prepareImages(image))[0];
2749	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImage);
2750	
2751	        // Run tokenization
2752	        const task_prompt = `<s_docvqa><s_question>${question}</s_question><s_answer>`;
2753	        const decoder_input_ids = this.tokenizer(task_prompt, {
2754	            add_special_tokens: false,
2755	            padding: true,
2756	            truncation: true,
2757	        }).input_ids;
2758	
2759	        // Run model
2760	        const output = await this.model.generate({
2761	            inputs: pixel_values,
2762	            // @ts-expect-error TS2339
2763	            max_length: this.model.config.decoder.max_position_embeddings,
2764	            decoder_input_ids,
2765	            ...generate_kwargs,
2766	        });
2767	
2768	        // Decode output
2769	        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output))[0];
2770	
2771	        // Parse answer
2772	        const match = decoded.match(/<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/);
2773	        let answer = null;
2774	        if (match && match.length >= 2) {
2775	            answer = match[1].trim();
2776	        }
2777	        return [{ answer }];
2778	    }
2779	}

718	async function genericTextToTextForward(self, {
719	    // Generic parameters:
720	    encode_function,
721	    merge_function,
722	    modality_input_name,
723	    modality_output_name,
724	
725	    // Produced by the tokenizer/processor:
726	    input_ids = null,
727	    attention_mask = null,
728	
729	    // Used during generation:
730	    position_ids = null,
731	    inputs_embeds = null,
732	    past_key_values = null,
733	
734	    // Generic generation parameters
735	    generation_config = null,
736	    logits_processor = null,
737	
738	    // Additional parameters
739	    ...kwargs
740	}) {
741	    const modality_values = kwargs[modality_input_name];
742	    if (!inputs_embeds) {
743	        // 1. Extract the text embeddings.
744	        inputs_embeds = await self.encode_text({ input_ids, ...kwargs });
745	
746	        // 2. Possibly, merge text and modality values
747	        if (modality_values && input_ids.dims[1] !== 1) {
748	            const modality_features = await encode_function({
749	                // Pass the modality values under its expected key.
750	                // The caller knows whether this is audio or image.
751	                [modality_input_name]: modality_values,
752	                ...kwargs
753	            });
754	            ({ inputs_embeds, attention_mask } = merge_function({
755	                [modality_output_name]: modality_features,
756	                inputs_embeds,
757	                input_ids,
758	                attention_mask,
759	            }));
760	
761	        } else if (past_key_values && modality_values && input_ids.dims[1] === 1) {
762	            // This branch handles the cache case.
763	            const target_length = input_ids.dims[1]; // always 1
764	            const past_length = Object.values(past_key_values)[0].dims.at(-2);
765	
766	            attention_mask = cat([
767	                ones([input_ids.dims[0], past_length]),
768	                attention_mask.slice(null, [attention_mask.dims[1] - target_length, attention_mask.dims[1]]),
769	            ], 1);
770	        }
771	    }
772	
773	    if (!position_ids) {
774	
775	        if (self.config.model_type === 'qwen2_vl') {
776	            // Special case for qwen2_vl models
777	            // @ts-ignore
778	            const { image_grid_thw, video_grid_thw } = kwargs;
779	            [position_ids] = self.get_rope_index(input_ids, image_grid_thw, video_grid_thw, attention_mask)
780	        }
781	    }
782	
783	    // 3. Call the decoder forward using the updated inputs.
784	    const outputs = await decoderForward(self, {
785	        inputs_embeds,
786	        past_key_values,
787	        attention_mask,
788	        position_ids,
789	        generation_config,
790	        logits_processor,
791	    }, true);
792	    return outputs;
793	}

3	 * A base class for creating callable objects.
4	 * See [here](https://stackoverflow.com/q/76073890) for more information.
5	 * 
6	 * @type {new () => {(...args: any[]): any, _call(...args: any[]): any}}
7	 */
8	export const Callable = /** @type {any} */ (class {
9	    /**
10	    * Creates a new instance of the Callable class.
11	    */
12	    constructor() {
13	        /**
14	         * Creates a closure that delegates to a private method '_call' with the given arguments.
15	         * @type {any}
16	         * @param {...any} args Zero or more arguments to pass to the '_call' method.
17	         * @returns {*} The result of calling the '_call' method.
18	         */
19	        let closure = function (...args) {
20	            return closure._call(...args)
21	        }
22	        return Object.setPrototypeOf(closure, new.target.prototype)
23	    }
24	
25	    /**
26	     * This method should be implemented in subclasses to provide the
27	     * functionality of the callable object.
28	     *
29	     * @param {any[]} args
30	     * @throws {Error} If the subclass does not implement the `_call` method.
31	     */
32	    _call(...args) {
33	        throw Error('Must implement _call method in subclass')
34	    }
35	});

**Workflow:**
1. **Task Alias Resolution** (line 3467): Applies TASK_ALIASES mappings to support alternative task names
2. **Pipeline Lookup** (line 3470): Splits the task by underscore and looks up in SUPPORTED_TASKS
3. **Error Handling** (lines 3471-3473): Throws error if task is unsupported
4. **Model Selection** (lines 3476-3479): Uses provided model or defaults to SUPPORTED_TASKS[task].default.model
5. **Options Preparation** (lines 3481-3493): Collates all pretrained loading options
6. **Dynamic Class Loading** (lines 3495-3502): Creates a Map of component classes (tokenizer, model, processor) and calls `loadItems()` to load them in parallel
7. **Status Callback** (lines 3505-3509): Dispatches a 'ready' status callback with task and model info
8. **Instantiation** (lines 3511-3512): Instantiates and returns the appropriate pipeline class with loaded components

**Helper Function - loadItems()** (lines 3523-3580):
- Loads tokenizers, models, and processors in parallel
- Handles arrays of model classes by trying each in sequence
- Catches specific errors ("Unsupported model type", "Could not locate file") and tries alternatives
- Returns object with loaded components keyed by name

### 5. **TASK_ALIASES Mapping**

**Location:** Lines 3394-3403 in `src/pipelines.js`


**JSDoc Type Annotations in Source:**

The library uses extensive JSDoc for type information (lines 3405-3412):
```javascript

 * @typedef {{[K in TaskType]: InstanceType<typeof SUPPORTED_TASKS[K]["pipeline"]>}} SupportedTasks
 * @typedef {{[K in AliasType]: InstanceType<typeof SUPPORTED_TASKS[TASK_ALIASES[K]]["pipeline"]>}} AliasTasks
 * @typedef {SupportedTasks & AliasTasks} AllTasks
 */
```

### 7. **Test File Locations**

**Main Pipeline Tests:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507986269-f82gsu/tests/pipelines.test.js` - Main test harness using `collect_and_execute_pipeline_tests()`

**Individual Pipeline Test Files** (tests/pipelines/):

- `test_pipelines_image_segmentation.js`

- `test_pipelines_text2text_generation.js`
- And more...

**Test Pattern:** Tests instantiate pipelines using the factory function and verify output shapes and types match expected values.

### 8. **Pipeline Base Class**

**Location:** Lines 164-185 in `src/pipelines.js`

All pipelines inherit from the `Pipeline` class which:
- Takes a constructor argument object with: `task`, `model`, `tokenizer`, `processor`
- Extends `Callable` (utility class making pipelines directly callable)
- Provides a `dispose()` method to cleanup resources

---

## Summary

The Transformers.js library uses a **registry pattern** with:
- A frozen `SUPPORTED_TASKS` object mapping task names to their components and configuration
- An async `pipeline()` factory function that orchestrates loading of models, tokenizers, and processors
- 26 supported task types with optional aliases for backward compatibility
- JSDoc-based type annotations that compile to TypeScript definitions at build time
- Parallel loading of all components for performance
- Support for fallback model classes when the primary choice isn't available

/**
 * @file Pipelines provide a high-level, easy to use, API for running machine learning models.
 *
 * **Example:** Instantiate pipeline using the `pipeline` function.
 * ```javascript
 * import { pipeline } from '@huggingface/transformers';
 *
 * const classifier = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis');
 * const output = await classifier('I love transformers!');
 * // [{'label': 'POSITIVE', 'score': 0.999817686}]
 * ```
 *
 * @module pipelines
 */

import {
    AutoTokenizer,
    PreTrainedTokenizer,
} from './tokenizers.js';

    AutoModel,
    AutoModelForSequenceClassification,

    AutoModelForVision2Seq,
    AutoModelForImageClassification,
    AutoModelForImageSegmentation,
    AutoModelForSemanticSegmentation,
    AutoModelForUniversalSegmentation,
    AutoModelForObjectDetection,
    AutoModelForZeroShotObjectDetection,
    AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering,
    AutoModelForImageToImage,
    AutoModelForDepthEstimation,
    AutoModelForImageFeatureExtraction,
    PreTrainedModel,
} from './models.js';

    AutoProcessor,
} from './models/auto/processing_auto.js';

1951	 * @returns {Promise<ImageToTextOutput|ImageToTextOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the generated text(s).
1952	 *
1953	 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & ImageToTextPipelineCallback & Disposable} ImageToTextPipelineType
1954	 */
1955	
1956	/**
1957	 * Image To Text pipeline using a `AutoModelForVision2Seq`. This pipeline predicts a caption for a given image.
1958	 *
1959	 * **Example:** Generate a caption for an image w/ `Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning`.
1960	 * ```javascript
1961	 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/vit-gpt2-image-captioning');
1962	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
1963	 * const output = await captioner(url);
1964	 * // [{ generated_text: 'a cat laying on a couch with another cat' }]
1965	 * ```
1966	 *
1967	 * **Example:** Optical Character Recognition (OCR) w/ `Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten`.
1968	 * ```javascript
1969	 * const captioner = await pipeline('image-to-text', 'Xenova/trocr-small-handwritten');
1970	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/handwriting.jpg';
1971	 * const output = await captioner(url);
1972	 * // [{ generated_text: 'Mr. Brown commented icily.' }]
1973	 * ```
1974	 */
1975	export class ImageToTextPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => ImageToTextPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
1976	
1977	    /**
1978	     * Create a new ImageToTextPipeline.
1979	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
1980	     */
1981	    constructor(options) {
1982	        super(options);
1983	    }
1984	
1985	    /** @type {ImageToTextPipelineCallback} */
1986	    async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {}) {
1987	
1988	        const isBatched = Array.isArray(images);
1989	        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
1990	
1991	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImages);
1992	
1993	        const toReturn = [];
1994	        for (const batch of pixel_values) {
1995	            batch.dims = [1, ...batch.dims]
1996	            const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: batch, ...generate_kwargs });
1997	            const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output), {
1998	                skip_special_tokens: true,
1999	            }).map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }))
2000	            toReturn.push(decoded);
2001	        }
2002	
2003	        return isBatched ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
2004	    }
2005	}

2028	 * **Example:** Classify an image.

2030	 * const classifier = await pipeline('image-classification', 'Xenova/vit-base-patch16-224');
2031	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/tiger.jpg';
2032	 * const output = await classifier(url);
2033	 * // [
2034	 * //   { label: 'tiger, Panthera tigris', score: 0.632695734500885 },

2037	 *
2038	 * **Example:** Classify an image and return top `n` classes.

2718	/**
2719	 * Document Question Answering pipeline using any `AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering`.
2720	 * The inputs/outputs are similar to the (extractive) question answering pipeline; however,
2721	 * the pipeline takes an image (and optional OCR'd words/boxes) as input instead of text context.
2722	 *
2723	 * **Example:** Answer questions about a document with `Xenova/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa`.
2724	 * ```javascript
2725	 * const qa_pipeline = await pipeline('document-question-answering', 'Xenova/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa');
2726	 * const image = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/invoice.png';
2727	 * const question = 'What is the invoice number?';
2728	 * const output = await qa_pipeline(image, question);
2729	 * // [{ answer: 'us-001' }]
2730	 * ```
2731	 */
2732	export class DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
2733	
2734	    /**
2735	     * Create a new DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline.
2736	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
2737	     */
2738	    constructor(options) {
2739	        super(options);
2740	    }
2741	
2742	    /** @type {DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback} */
2743	    async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {}) {
2744	
2745	        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1
2746	
2747	        // Preprocess image
2748	        const preparedImage = (await prepareImages(image))[0];
2749	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImage);
2750	
2751	        // Run tokenization
2752	        const task_prompt = `<s_docvqa><s_question>${question}</s_question><s_answer>`;
2753	        const decoder_input_ids = this.tokenizer(task_prompt, {
2754	            add_special_tokens: false,
2755	            padding: true,
2756	            truncation: true,
2757	        }).input_ids;
2758	
2759	        // Run model
2760	        const output = await this.model.generate({
2761	            inputs: pixel_values,
2762	            // @ts-expect-error TS2339
2763	            max_length: this.model.config.decoder.max_position_embeddings,
2764	            decoder_input_ids,
2765	            ...generate_kwargs,
2766	        });
2767	
2768	        // Decode output
2769	        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output))[0];
2770	
2771	        // Parse answer
2772	        const match = decoded.match(/<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/);
2773	        let answer = null;
2774	        if (match && match.length >= 2) {
2775	            answer = match[1].trim();
2776	        }
2777	        return [{ answer }];
2778	    }
2779	}

2718	/**
2719	 * Document Question Answering pipeline using any `AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering`.
2720	 * The inputs/outputs are similar to the (extractive) question answering pipeline; however,
2721	 * the pipeline takes an image (and optional OCR'd words/boxes) as input instead of text context.
2722	 *
2723	 * **Example:** Answer questions about a document with `Xenova/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa`.
2724	 * ```javascript
2725	 * const qa_pipeline = await pipeline('document-question-answering', 'Xenova/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa');
2726	 * const image = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/invoice.png';
2727	 * const question = 'What is the invoice number?';
2728	 * const output = await qa_pipeline(image, question);
2729	 * // [{ answer: 'us-001' }]
2730	 * ```
2731	 */
2732	export class DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
2733	
2734	    /**
2735	     * Create a new DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline.
2736	     * @param {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
2737	     */
2738	    constructor(options) {
2739	        super(options);
2740	    }
2741	
2742	    /** @type {DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback} */
2743	    async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {}) {
2744	
2745	        // NOTE: For now, we only support a batch size of 1
2746	
2747	        // Preprocess image
2748	        const preparedImage = (await prepareImages(image))[0];
2749	        const { pixel_values } = await this.processor(preparedImage);
2750	
2751	        // Run tokenization
2752	        const task_prompt = `<s_docvqa><s_question>${question}</s_question><s_answer>`;
2753	        const decoder_input_ids = this.tokenizer(task_prompt, {
2754	            add_special_tokens: false,
2755	            padding: true,
2756	            truncation: true,
2757	        }).input_ids;
2758	
2759	        // Run model
2760	        const output = await this.model.generate({
2761	            inputs: pixel_values,
2762	            // @ts-expect-error TS2339
2763	            max_length: this.model.config.decoder.max_position_embeddings,
2764	            decoder_input_ids,
2765	            ...generate_kwargs,
2766	        });
2767	
2768	        // Decode output
2769	        const decoded = this.tokenizer.batch_decode(/** @type {Tensor} */(output))[0];
2770	
2771	        // Parse answer
2772	        const match = decoded.match(/<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/);
2773	        let answer = null;
2774	        if (match && match.length >= 2) {
2775	            answer = match[1].trim();
2776	        }
2777	        return [{ answer }];
2778	    }
2779	}

105	/**
106	 * Prepare audios for further tasks.
107	 * @param {AudioPipelineInputs} audios audios to prepare.
108	 * @param {number} sampling_rate sampling rate of the audios.
109	 * @returns {Promise<Float32Array[]>} The preprocessed audio data.
110	 * @private
111	 */
112	async function prepareAudios(audios, sampling_rate) {
113	    if (!Array.isArray(audios)) {
114	        audios = [audios];
115	    }
116	
117	    return await Promise.all(audios.map(x => {
118	        if (typeof x === 'string' || x instanceof URL) {
119	            return read_audio(x, sampling_rate);
120	        } else if (x instanceof Float64Array) {
121	            return new Float32Array(x);
122	        }
123	        return x;
124	    }));
125	}

135	/**
136	 * Helper function to convert list [xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax] into object { "xmin": xmin, ... }
137	 * @param {number[]} box The bounding box as a list.
138	 * @param {boolean} asInteger Whether to cast to integers.
139	 * @returns {BoundingBox} The bounding box as an object.
140	 * @private
141	 */
142	function get_bounding_box(box, asInteger) {
143	    if (asInteger) {
144	        box = box.map(x => x | 0);
145	    }
146	    const [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] = box;
147	
148	    return { xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax };
149	}

input.content:
# Plan: Add `image-text-to-text` Pipeline to Transformers.js

## Context

The Transformers.js library already has full model-layer support for image-text-to-text tasks (`AutoModelForImageTextToText` in `src/models.js` with 11 model architectures including LLaVA, Qwen2-VL, Florence2, etc.), but lacks a corresponding high-level pipeline class. This means users cannot use the simple `pipeline('image-text-to-text', model)` API for these models. Adding this pipeline bridges the gap, enabling a clean API for multimodal image+text → text generation.

## File to Modify

**`src/pipelines.js`** — the only file that needs changes (4 edits).

## Changes

### 1. Add Import — line 43

Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the import block from `./models.js`:

```javascript
    AutoModelForImageFeatureExtraction,
    AutoModelForImageTextToText,    // ← ADD
    PreTrainedModel,
```

### 2. Add JSDoc Typedefs + Pipeline Class — after line 2005

Insert immediately after the closing `}` of `ImageToTextPipeline` (line 2005), before the `ImageClassificationSingle` typedef block (line 2007).

**Typedefs** (following exact pattern of `ImageToTextPipeline`):
- `ImageTextToTextSingle` — `{ generated_text: string }`
- `ImageTextToTextOutput` — `ImageTextToTextSingle[]`
- `ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback` — `(images, text?, options?) => Promise<Output|Output[]>`
- `ImageTextToTextPipelineType` — `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & Callback & Disposable`

**Class: `ImageTextToTextPipeline`**
- Extends `Pipeline` via `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` (needs model, tokenizer, AND processor)
- `_call(images, text = null, generate_kwargs = {})`:
  1. Check `isBatched = Array.isArray(images)`
  2. `prepareImages(images)` → `RawImage[]`
  3. Call `this.processor(preparedImages, text)` — passes images + text to model-specific processor (e.g., `LlavaProcessor._call(images, text)` which expands image tokens and tokenizes text, returning `{ pixel_values, input_ids, attention_mask }`)
  4. Call `this.model.generate({ ...processorOutput, ...generate_kwargs })`
  5. Decode with `this.tokenizer.batch_decode(output, { skip_special_tokens: true })`
  6. Return `[{ generated_text: "..." }]` (single) or `[[{ generated_text }]]` (batched)

### 3. Add SUPPORTED_TASKS Entry — after line 3255

Insert after the `"image-to-text"` entry for logical grouping:

```javascript
"image-text-to-text": {
    "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
    "pipeline": ImageTextToTextPipeline,
    "model": AutoModelForImageTextToText,
    "processor": AutoProcessor,
    "default": {
        "model": "Xenova/tiny-random-LlavaForConditionalGeneration",
    },
    "type": "multimodal",
},
```

### 4. Update `pipeline()` JSDoc — after line 3427

Add to the task list in the `pipeline()` function's JSDoc:

```
 *  - `"image-text-to-text"`: will return a `ImageTextToTextPipeline`.
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **`_call` signature is `(images, text, generate_kwargs)`** — images first matches `LlavaProcessor._call(images, text)` convention and is consistent with other image pipelines in the codebase.

2. **Processor handles image+text fusion** — the pipeline delegates combining image pixels and text tokens to the model-specific processor (e.g., `LlavaProcessor` expands `<image>` tokens and merges inputs). The pipeline does NOT manually handle this.

3. **Single `model.generate()` call with full processor output** — unlike `ImageToTextPipeline` which loops over individual pixel batches, this pipeline passes the complete processor output (including `input_ids` + `pixel_values`) since the processor already handles the multimodal combination.

4. **Default model: `Xenova/tiny-random-LlavaForConditionalGeneration`** — a tiny test model already used in the repo's LLaVA tests. Can be upgraded to a production model later.

## Verification

1. **Import check**: Confirm `AutoModelForImageTextToText` is already exported from `src/models.js` (line 8736 — confirmed it exists)
2. **Instantiation**: `const pipe = await pipeline('image-text-to-text', 'Xenova/tiny-random-LlavaForConditionalGeneration')` should return an `ImageTextToTextPipeline` instance
3. **Run inference**: `await pipe(image, 'Describe this image')` should return `[{ generated_text: '...' }]`
4. **Test file** (optional follow-up): Create `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_text_to_text.js` following the pattern in `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_to_text.js` — it will be auto-discovered by the test runner in `tests/test_utils.js`

## Key Design Decisions

1. **`_call` signature is `(images, text, generate_kwargs)`** — images first matches `LlavaProcessor._call(images, text)` convention and is consistent with other image pipelines in the codebase.

2. **Processor handles image+text fusion** — the pipeline delegates combining image pixels and text tokens to the model-specific processor (e.g., `LlavaProcessor` expands `<image>` tokens and merges inputs). The pipeline does NOT manually handle this.

3. **Single `model.generate()` call with full processor output** — unlike `ImageToTextPipeline` which loops over individual pixel batches, this pipeline passes the complete processor output (including `input_ids` + `pixel_values`) since the processor already handles the multimodal combination.

4. **Default model: `Xenova/tiny-random-LlavaForConditionalGeneration`** — a tiny test model already used in the repo's LLaVA tests. Can be upgraded to a production model later.

## Verification

1. **Import check**: Confirm `AutoModelForImageTextToText` is already exported from `src/models.js` (line 8736 — confirmed it exists)
2. **Instantiation**: `const pipe = await pipeline('image-text-to-text', 'Xenova/tiny-random-LlavaForConditionalGeneration')` should return an `ImageTextToTextPipeline` instance
3. **Run inference**: `await pipe(image, 'Describe this image')` should return `[{ generated_text: '...' }]`
4. **Test file** (optional follow-up): Create `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_text_to_text.js` following the pattern in `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_to_text.js` — it will be auto-discovered by the test runner in `tests/test_utils.js`

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: Add `image-text-to-text` Pipeline to Transformers.js

## Context

The Transformers.js library already has full model-layer support for image-text-to-text tasks (`AutoModelForImageTextToText` in `src/models.js` with 11 model architectures including LLaVA, Qwen2-VL, Florence2, etc.), but lacks a corresponding high-level pipeline class. This means users cannot use the simple `pipeline('image-text-to-text', model)` API for these models. Adding this pipeline bridges the gap, enabling a clean API for multimodal image+text → text generation.

## File to Modify

**`src/pipelines.js`** — the only file that needs changes (4 edits).

## Changes

### 1. Add Import — line 43

Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the import block from `./models.js`:

```javascript
    AutoModelForImageFeatureExtraction,
    AutoModelForImageTextToText,    // ← ADD
    PreTrainedModel,
```

### 2. Add JSDoc Typedefs + Pipeline Class — after line 2005

Insert immediately after the closing `}` of `ImageToTextPipeline` (line 2005), before the `ImageClassificationSingle` typedef block (line 2007).

**Typedefs** (following exact pattern of `ImageToTextPipeline`):
- `ImageTextToTextSingle` — `{ generated_text: string }`
- `ImageTextToTextOutput` — `ImageTextToTextSingle[]`
- `ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback` — `(images, text?, options?) => Promise<Output|Output[]>`
- `ImageTextToTextPipelineType` — `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & Callback & Disposable`

**Class: `ImageTextToTextPipeline`**
- Extends `Pipeline` via `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` (needs model, tokenizer, AND processor)
- `_call(images, text = null, generate_kwargs = {})`:
  1. Check `isBatched = Array.isArray(images)`
  2. `prepareImages(images)` → `RawImage[]`
  3. Call `this.processor(preparedImages, text)` — passes images + text to model-specific processor (e.g., `LlavaProcessor._call(images, text)` which expands image tokens and tokenizes text, returning `{ pixel_values, input_ids, attention_mask }`)
  4. Call `this.model.generate({ ...processorOutput, ...generate_kwargs })`
  5. Decode with `this.tokenizer.batch_decode(output, { skip_special_tokens: true })`
  6. Return `[{ generated_text: "..." }]` (single) or `[[{ generated_text }]]` (batched)

### 3. Add SUPPORTED_TASKS Entry — after line 3255

Insert after the `"image-to-text"` entry for logical grouping:

```javascript
"image-text-to-text": {
    "tokenizer": AutoTokenizer,
    "pipeline": ImageTextToTextPipeline,
    "model": AutoModelForImageTextToText,
    "processor": AutoProcessor,
    "default": {
        "model": "Xenova/tiny-random-LlavaForConditionalGeneration",
    },
    "type": "multimodal",
},
```

### 4. Update `pipeline()` JSDoc — after line 3427

Add to the task list in the `pipeline()` function's JSDoc:

```
 *  - `"image-text-to-text"`: will return a `ImageTextToTextPipeline`.
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **`_call` signature is `(images, text, generate_kwargs)`** — images first matches `LlavaProcessor._call(images, text)` convention and is consistent with other image pipelines in the codebase.

2. **Processor handles image+text fusion** — the pipeline delegates combining image pixels and text tokens to the model-specific processor (e.g., `LlavaProcessor` expands `<image>` tokens and merges inputs). The pipeline does NOT manually handle this.

3. **Single `model.generate()` call with full processor output** — unlike `ImageToTextPipeline` which loops over individual pixel batches, this pipeline passes the complete processor output (including `input_ids` + `pixel_values`) since the processor already handles the multimodal combination.

4. **Default model: `Xenova/tiny-random-LlavaForConditionalGeneration`** — a tiny test model already used in the repo's LLaVA tests. Can be upgraded to a production model later.

## Verification

1. **Import check**: Confirm `AutoModelForImageTextToText` is already exported from `src/models.js` (line 8736 — confirmed it exists)
2. **Instantiation**: `const pipe = await pipeline('image-text-to-text', 'Xenova/tiny-random-LlavaForConditionalGeneration')` should return an `ImageTextToTextPipeline` instance
3. **Run inference**: `await pipe(image, 'Describe this image')` should return `[{ generated_text: '...' }]`
4. **Test file** (optional follow-up): Create `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_text_to_text.js` following the pattern in `tests/pipelines/test_pipelines_image_to_text.js` — it will be auto-discovered by the test runner in `tests/test_utils.js`

## Comprehensive Report: Transformers.js Pipeline Architecture

Based on my thorough search of the Transformers.js library, here's a detailed analysis of the pipeline architecture:

---

### 1. Main Pipelines File Location and General Structure

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507986269-f82gsu/src/pipelines.js` (3,580 lines)

**General Structure**:
- Starts with a comprehensive JSDoc module definition and imports
- Defines helper functions (`prepareImages`, `prepareAudios`, `get_bounding_box`)
- Exports a base `Pipeline` class extending `Callable`
- Exports 26 pipeline-specific classes that extend Pipeline
- Defines `SUPPORTED_TASKS` object mapping task names to their implementations
- Defines `TASK_ALIASES` for backward compatibility
- Exports the main `pipeline()` factory function
- Includes helper function `loadItems()` for loading models/tokenizers/processors

**Key Pattern**: Pipeline classes use a sophisticated TypeScript-like JSDoc pattern where they extend Pipeline through a cast that includes type definitions:
```javascript
export class TextClassificationPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: TextPipelineConstructorArgs) => TextClassificationPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
```

---

### 2. Multimodal Pipeline Implementations

#### **ImageToTextPipeline**
- **Location**: Lines 1975-2005 in `src/pipelines.js`
- **Constructor**: Uses `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs` (tokenizer + processor + model)
- **Signature**: `async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {})`
- **Implementation Pattern**:
  ```javascript
  1. Prepare images with prepareImages()
  2. Process images with this.processor() → pixel_values
  3. For each batch:
     - Set batch dimensions [1, ...batch.dims]
     - Call model.generate() with pixel_values
     - Decode output with tokenizer.batch_decode()
  4. Return in format [{ generated_text: string }]
  ```
- **Model Used**: `AutoModelForVision2Seq`
- **Task Type**: "multimodal"

#### **DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline**
- **Location**: Lines 2732-2779 in `src/pipelines.js`
- **Constructor**: Uses `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs`
- **Signature**: `async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {})`
- **Implementation Pattern**:
  ```javascript
  1. Prepare single image with prepareImages()
  2. Process image with this.processor() → pixel_values
  3. Create task-specific prompt: "<s_docvqa><s_question>{question}</s_question><s_answer>"
  4. Tokenize prompt as decoder_input_ids
  5. Call model.generate() with both pixel_values and decoder_input_ids
  6. Decode output and parse answer from regex: /<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/
  7. Return [{ answer: string }]
  ```
- **Model Used**: `AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering`
- **Task Type**: "multimodal"

#### **ZeroShotImageClassificationPipeline** (Text+Image example)
- **Location**: Lines 2389-2444 in `src/pipelines.js`
- **Constructor**: Uses `TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs`
- **Signature**: `async _call(images, candidate_labels, { hypothesis_template = "This is a photo of {}" } = {})`
- **Implementation Pattern**:
  ```javascript
  1. Format candidate_labels using hypothesis_template
  2. Tokenize text with this.tokenizer()
  3. Process images with this.processor() → pixel_values
  4. Call model with both text_inputs and pixel_values
  5. Apply function_to_apply (sigmoid/softmax based on model type)
  6. For each image, compare logits with all labels
  7. Sort results by score and return
  ```
- **Unique Feature**: Handles different model types (siglip vs others) with conditional logic
- **Model Used**: `AutoModel`
- **Task Type**: "multimodal"

---

### 3. SUPPORTED_TASKS Mapping Definition

**Location**: Lines 3096-3390 in `src/pipelines.js`

**Structure**: A frozen object with task names as keys and configuration objects as values:

```javascript
const SUPPORTED_TASKS = Object.freeze({
    "<task-name>": {
        "tokenizer": <AutoTokenizer or null>,
        "pipeline": <PipelineClass>,
        "model": <AutoModelClass or [AutoModelClass, ...]>,
        "processor": <AutoProcessor or null or [AutoProcessor, null]>,
        "default": {
            "model": "<default-model-id>"
        },
        "type": "<text|audio|image|multimodal>"
    },
    // ... more tasks
})
```

**Example Entries**:
- `"image-to-text"`: Uses `AutoTokenizer`, `ImageToTextPipeline`, `AutoModelForVision2Seq`, `AutoProcessor`
- `"document-question-answering"`: Uses `AutoTokenizer`, `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`, `AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering`, `AutoProcessor`
- `"zero-shot-image-classification"`: Uses `AutoTokenizer`, `ZeroShotImageClassificationPipeline`, `AutoModel`, `AutoProcessor`
- `"image-classification"`: NO tokenizer, uses `ImageClassificationPipeline`, `AutoModelForImageClassification`, `AutoProcessor`

**TASK_ALIASES** (Lines 3394-3403):
Maps user-friendly names to actual task names (e.g., `"sentiment-analysis"` → `"text-classification"`, `"asr"` → `"automatic-speech-recognition"`)
Note: `"vqa"` → `"visual-question-answering"` is marked as TODO (not yet implemented)

---

### 4. AutoModelForImageTextToText Definition and Export

**Location in models.js**: Lines 8736-8738

```javascript
export class AutoModelForImageTextToText extends PretrainedMixin {
    static MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS = [MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES];
}
```

**Model Type Mapping** (Lines 8240-8252 in `src/models.js`):
```javascript
const MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES = new Map([
    ['llava', ['LlavaForConditionalGeneration', LlavaForConditionalGeneration]],
    ['llava_onevision', ['LlavaOnevisionForConditionalGeneration', ...]],
    ['moondream1', ['Moondream1ForConditionalGeneration', ...]],
    ['florence2', ['Florence2ForConditionalGeneration', ...]],
    ['qwen2-vl', ['Qwen2VLForConditionalGeneration', ...]],
    ['idefics3', ['Idefics3ForConditionalGeneration', ...]],
    ['smolvlm', ['SmolVLMForConditionalGeneration', ...]],
    ['paligemma', ['PaliGemmaForConditionalGeneration', ...]],
    ['llava_qwen2', ['LlavaQwen2ForCausalLM', ...]],
    ['gemma3n', ['Gemma3nForConditionalGeneration', ...]],
    ['mistral3', ['Mistral3ForConditionalGeneration', ...]],
]);
```

**Export**: Located in `src/models.js` and imported into `src/pipelines.js` (line 33)

---

### 5. Type Definitions and JSDoc Patterns

**Constructor Type Patterns** (Lines 188-216):

```javascript
/**
 * @typedef {Object} ModelTokenizerConstructorArgs
 * @property {string} task The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
 * @property {PreTrainedModel} model The model used by the pipeline.
 * @property {PreTrainedTokenizer} tokenizer The tokenizer used by the pipeline.
 * @typedef {ModelTokenizerConstructorArgs} TextPipelineConstructorArgs
 */

/**
 * @typedef {Object} ModelProcessorConstructorArgs
 * @property {string} task The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
 * @property {PreTrainedModel} model The model used by the pipeline.
 * @property {Processor} processor The processor used by the pipeline.
 * @typedef {ModelProcessorConstructorArgs} AudioPipelineConstructorArgs
 * @typedef {ModelProcessorConstructorArgs} ImagePipelineConstructorArgs
 */

/**
 * @typedef {Object} ModelTokenizerProcessorConstructorArgs
 * @property {string} task The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
 * @property {PreTrainedModel} model The model used by the pipeline.
 * @property {PreTrainedTokenizer} tokenizer The tokenizer used by the pipeline.
 * @property {Processor} processor The processor used by the pipeline.
 * @typedef {ModelTokenizerProcessorConstructorArgs} TextAudioPipelineConstructorArgs
 * @typedef {ModelTokenizerProcessorConstructorArgs} TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs
 */
```

**Input/Output Type Patterns**:

All pipelines follow this pattern:
1. **Input typedef** (e.g., `ImageToTextSingle`, `DocumentQuestionAnsweringSingle`)
2. **Output typedef** (e.g., `ImageToTextOutput = ImageToTextSingle[]`)
3. **Options typedef** (e.g., `ObjectDetectionPipelineOptions` with optional parameters)
4. **Callback typedef** defining the `_call()` method signature
5. **Pipeline type typedef** combining: `ConstructorArgs & CallbackType & Disposable`

**Example for DocumentQuestionAnswering**:
```javascript
/**
 * @typedef {Object} DocumentQuestionAnsweringSingle
 * @property {string} answer The generated text.
 * @typedef {DocumentQuestionAnsweringSingle[]} DocumentQuestionAnsweringOutput
 *
 * @callback DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback Answer the question given as input by using the document.
 * @param {ImageInput} image The image of the document to use.
 * @param {string} question A question to ask of the document.
 * @param {Partial<import('./generation/configuration_utils.js').GenerationConfig>} [options] Additional keyword arguments to pass along to the generate method of the model.
 * @returns {Promise<DocumentQuestionAnsweringOutput|DocumentQuestionAnsweringOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the answer(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback & Disposable} DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineType
 */
```

**Disposable Type** (Lines 152-158):
```javascript
/**
 * @callback DisposeType Disposes the item.
 * @returns {Promise<void>} A promise that resolves when the item has been disposed.
 *
 * @typedef {Object} Disposable
 * @property {DisposeType} dispose A promise that resolves when the pipeline has been disposed.
 */
```

**Generic Input Types** (Lines 81-104):
```javascript
/**
 * @typedef {string | RawImage | URL | Blob | HTMLCanvasElement | OffscreenCanvas} ImageInput
 * @typedef {ImageInput|ImageInput[]} ImagePipelineInputs
 */

/**
 * @typedef {string | URL | Float32Array | Float64Array} AudioInput
 * @typedef {AudioInput|AudioInput[]} AudioPipelineInputs
 */
```

---

### 6. Base Pipeline Class Structure

**Location**: Lines 164-185 in `src/pipelines.js`

```javascript
export class Pipeline extends Callable {
    /**
     * Create a new Pipeline.
     * @param {Object} options An object containing the following properties:
     * @param {string} [options.task] The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
     * @param {PreTrainedModel} [options.model] The model used by the pipeline.
     * @param {PreTrainedTokenizer} [options.tokenizer=null] The tokenizer used by the pipeline (if any).
     * @param {Processor} [options.processor=null] The processor used by the pipeline (if any).
     */
    constructor({ task, model, tokenizer = null, processor = null }) {
        super();
        this.task = task;
        this.model = model;
        this.tokenizer = tokenizer;
        this.processor = processor;
    }

    /** @type {DisposeType} */
    async dispose() {
        await this.model.dispose();
    }
}
```

---

### 7. Pipeline Factory Function

**Location**: Lines 3446-3513 in `src/pipelines.js`

The main entry point that:
1. Resolves task aliases
2. Looks up task in `SUPPORTED_TASKS`
3. Loads models/tokenizers/processors in parallel using `loadItems()`
4. Instantiates and returns the appropriate pipeline class

```javascript
export async function pipeline(
    task,
    model = null,
    {
        progress_callback = null,
        config = null,
        cache_dir = null,
        local_files_only = false,
        revision = 'main',
        device = null,
        dtype = null,
        subfolder = 'onnx',
        use_external_data_format = null,
        model_file_name = null,
        session_options = {},
    } = {}
)
```

---

### Key Architectural Insights

1. **PretrainedMixin Pattern**: All AutoModel classes extend `PretrainedMixin` (defined in models.js line 7843) which provides the `from_pretrained()` static method for loading models
2. **Flexible Model Selection**: SUPPORTED_TASKS can specify single models, arrays of models (tries each until one works), or null (optional)
3. **Task-Specific Subtasks**: The pipeline function splits on underscore to handle translation subtasks (e.g., "translation_en_to_fr")
4. **Parallel Loading**: The `loadItems()` function loads all required components (model, tokenizer, processor) in parallel for performance
5. **Generator/Generation Support**: Multimodal pipelines like ImageToText use the `model.generate()` method which supports generation configs
6. **No VQA Yet**: Visual Question Answering is marked as TODO in TASK_ALIASES but not yet fully implemented

All files are located in the working directory: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507986269-f82gsu/`

```javascript
 * @returns {Promise<DocumentQuestionAnsweringOutput|DocumentQuestionAnsweringOutput[]>} An object (or array of objects) containing the answer(s).
 *
 * @typedef {TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs & DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineCallback & Disposable} DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipelineType
 */
```

**Disposable Type** (Lines 152-158):
```javascript
/**
 * @callback DisposeType Disposes the item.
 * @returns {Promise<void>} A promise that resolves when the item has been disposed.
 *
 * @typedef {Object} Disposable
 * @property {DisposeType} dispose A promise that resolves when the pipeline has been disposed.
 */
```

**Generic Input Types** (Lines 81-104):
```javascript
/**
 * @typedef {string | RawImage | URL | Blob | HTMLCanvasElement | OffscreenCanvas} ImageInput
 * @typedef {ImageInput|ImageInput[]} ImagePipelineInputs
 */

/**
 * @typedef {string | URL | Float32Array | Float64Array} AudioInput
 * @typedef {AudioInput|AudioInput[]} AudioPipelineInputs
 */
```

---

### 6. Base Pipeline Class Structure

**Location**: Lines 164-185 in `src/pipelines.js`

```javascript
export class Pipeline extends Callable {
    /**
     * Create a new Pipeline.
     * @param {Object} options An object containing the following properties:
     * @param {string} [options.task] The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
     * @param {PreTrainedModel} [options.model] The model used by the pipeline.
     * @param {PreTrainedTokenizer} [options.tokenizer=null] The tokenizer used by the pipeline (if any).
     * @param {Processor} [options.processor=null] The processor used by the pipeline (if any).
     */
    constructor({ task, model, tokenizer = null, processor = null }) {
        super();
        this.task = task;
        this.model = model;
        this.tokenizer = tokenizer;
        this.processor = processor;
    }

    /** @type {DisposeType} */
    async dispose() {
        await this.model.dispose();
    }
}
```

---

### 7. Pipeline Factory Function

**Location**: Lines 3446-3513 in `src/pipelines.js`

The main entry point that:
1. Resolves task aliases
2. Looks up task in `SUPPORTED_TASKS`
3. Loads models/tokenizers/processors in parallel using `loadItems()`
4. Instantiates and returns the appropriate pipeline class

```javascript
export async function pipeline(
    task,
    model = null,
    {
        progress_callback = null,
        config = null,
        cache_dir = null,
        local_files_only = false,
        revision = 'main',
        device = null,
        dtype = null,
        subfolder = 'onnx',
        use_external_data_format = null,
        model_file_name = null,
        session_options = {},
    } = {}
)
```

---

### Key Architectural Insights

1. **PretrainedMixin Pattern**: All AutoModel classes extend `PretrainedMixin` (defined in models.js line 7843) which provides the `from_pretrained()` static method for loading models
2. **Flexible Model Selection**: SUPPORTED_TASKS can specify single models, arrays of models (tries each until one works), or null (optional)
3. **Task-Specific Subtasks**: The pipeline function splits on underscore to handle translation subtasks (e.g., "translation_en_to_fr")
4. **Parallel Loading**: The `loadItems()` function loads all required components (model, tokenizer, processor) in parallel for performance
5. **Generator/Generation Support**: Multimodal pipelines like ImageToText use the `model.generate()` method which supports generation configs
6. **No VQA Yet**: Visual Question Answering is marked as TODO in TASK_ALIASES but not yet fully implemented

All files are located in the working directory: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775507986269-f82gsu/`
